commentary_text: The sportsbook consensus assigns Bosnia and Herzegovina a 67% win probability versus the model's 46%, a 21-percentage-point gap favoring the underdog read from the statistical forecast. This divergence suggests the market may be pricing in information the model cannot access—recent form trends, injury status, or lineup changes—that substantially favors Bosnia and Herzegovina's chances. The model's Elo and goal distribution remain locked to historical data; real-money markets continuously incorporate breaking team news and bettors' latest intelligence. The dashboard surfaces this gap as a signal worth monitoring rather than a judgment on which assessment will prove correct.
